metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact mechanism (e.g., a Compose `secrets` mount, an entrypoint script, a Terraform `aws_instance` user-data step, or an Ansible task) by which `secure.config` is written from SSM to `$GERRIT_SITE/etc/` before Gerrit starts, and document it in the runbook and in `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md`.
findings.finding: The mechanism by which `secure.config` (containing the OAuth client-secret) is materialized from SSM onto the live host is left implicit ('materialized from SSM like the S5 deploy key'), with no specified script, Compose secret mount, or init-container step; if the mechanism doesn't already exist for Gerrit, the consumer (the plugin) will silently fail to authenticate.
findings.location: Config (the target state) — `etc/secure.config` description; Deliverables → In-repo → install-plugins scaffolding

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Scope the automation gap: either (a) add updating `compose-up.sh`/`fetch-secrets.sh` to handle the two new OAuth SSM params and write them into `gerrit.config`/`secure.config` as an in-repo deliverable, or (b) explicitly document that the OAuth-config materialization is permanently operator-manual and explain how a fresh redeploy avoids reverting `gerrit.config` to the PoC value.
findings.finding: The runbook describes `compose-up.sh` writing the OAuth `client-id` into `site/etc/gerrit.config` and the `client-secret` into `site/etc/secure.config` (from SSM), but the existing `compose-up.sh` and `fetch-secrets.sh` have no such logic — and updating them to do so is not listed as an in-repo deliverable. This introduces a second secrets-materialization pathway (SSM → gerrit.config/secure.config) alongside the established one (SSM → fetch-secrets.sh → .env → container env vars), without specifying whether it will be automated or remain a permanently-manual operator step.
findings.location: infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md Step 2; Deliverables — In-repo section; infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh; infra/scripts/compose-up.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Extend `fetch-secrets.sh` to read `github-oauth-client-id` (non-secret, or SecureString is fine) and `github-oauth-client-secret`, and extend `compose-up.sh` (after the `cp gerrit.config` step) to substitute the `[auth]` block and write `secure.config` — mirroring the existing `materialize-deploy-key.sh` pattern. Add both SSM param names to `ssm.tf`'s `rebar_secret_params` list and list this scripting work as an in-repo deliverable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add oauth.jar download + sha256-verify + install logic to `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh` (mirroring the existing events-log pattern) so AC2's 'wired into the plugin install path' claim is true against the actual script.
findings.finding: The plan's AC2 asserts the gerrit-oauth-provider jar is 'wired into the plugin install path', but `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh` — the only plugin installation script in the repo — handles only `events-log`. No oauth.jar download, sha256-verify, or install logic exists anywhere in `infra/scripts/` or `infra/gerrit/`. The claim of 'install-plugins scaffolding' for the oauth plugin is unverified against the actual codebase.
findings.location: Deliverables / In-repo section: 'install-plugins scaffolding'; AC2: 'wired into the plugin install path'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend install-plugins.sh with a second block: OAUTH_URL, OAUTH_SHA256 (to be filled once fetched), download to tmp, verify sha256, mv to $PLUGINS_DIR/oauth.jar — identical pattern to the events-log block already there.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either update `fetch-secrets.sh` + `compose-up.sh` to actually fetch `github_oauth_client_id`/`github_oauth_client_secret` from SSM and inject them into `site/etc/gerrit.config` + `site/etc/secure.config`, or reword the runbook Step 2 to describe the injection as a manual operator action (not a script action) so the documented procedure matches reality.
findings.finding: The runbook (Step 2) asserts '`fetch-secrets.sh` materializes these [github_oauth_client_id / github_oauth_client_secret] into the container .env' and '`compose-up.sh` writes the client-id into site/etc/gerrit.config and the client-secret into site/etc/secure.config'. Neither script does this. `fetch-secrets.sh` fetches exactly 4 hardcoded params (anthropic-api-key, mcp-hmac-signing-key, gerrit-admin-password, gerrit-bot-token) and `compose-up.sh` only `cp`s gerrit.config verbatim — no oauth credential injection logic exists in either script.
findings.location: infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md Step 2; plan Deliverables 'In-repo' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh` (or create a companion script) to download, sha256-verify, and install `gerrit-oauth-provider` (`oauth.jar`), matching the events-log pattern — or clearly document that this is a manual step not covered by the script.
findings.finding: `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh` is named in the runbook as the mechanism to fetch and install `oauth.jar`, but the script only handles `events-log` — it contains no logic for `gerrit-oauth-provider`/`oauth.jar`. The in-repo AC states the jar is 'wired into the plugin install path', which this script is supposed to fulfil.
findings.location: Deliverables / In-repo AC: 'wired into the plugin install path'; infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md Step 3; infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Add a second download/verify/install block to `install-plugins.sh` with env-overridable `OAUTH_JAR_URL` and `OAUTH_JAR_SHA256` variables (parallel to the events-log block), and record the final sha256 in `plugins/README.md`. Update the runbook to call out the script explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either update `fetch-secrets.sh` and `compose-up.sh` to fetch and inject the OAuth client-id/secret into the live `gerrit.config`/`secure.config` (matching the runbook description), or revise the runbook Step 2 to replace the fictional automation with explicit manual commands an operator must run.
findings.finding: The runbook (Step 2) states that `fetch-secrets.sh` materializes `github_oauth_client_id`/`github_oauth_client_secret` into the container `.env` and that `compose-up.sh` writes the client-id into `site/etc/gerrit.config` and the client-secret into `site/etc/secure.config`. Neither script does this: `fetch-secrets.sh` fetches exactly four hardcoded SSM leaves (none are OAuth creds), and `compose-up.sh` only copies the committed `gerrit.config` wholesale. These are unenumerated consumers that the plan implies will be updated.
findings.location: infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md Step 2; infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh; infra/scripts/compose-up.sh

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document (in the runbook or a script update) how `infra/scripts/bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh` must be updated or replaced after the OAUTH switch, since its DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT dev-login step does not work under `auth.type = OAUTH`.
findings.finding: `infra/scripts/bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh` uses a `DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT`-specific mechanism (a `GET /login/...?account_id=1000000` HTTP call) to create account 1000000 before registering its SSH key. After switching to `auth.type = OAUTH`, this mechanism no longer works — the dev-login endpoint is gone. The plan does not name this script as a consumer requiring update, even though it is the canonical fresh-deploy admin bootstrap tool.
findings.location: infra/scripts/bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh lines 38-39; Deliverables (no mention of bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either update `fetch-secrets.sh` and `compose-up.sh` to actually materialize the `github_oauth_client_id`/`github_oauth_client_secret` SSM params into `gerrit.config`/`secure.config` as the runbook describes, or revise the runbook's Step 2 to accurately reflect that these are manual operator steps (with exact commands) rather than automated script behavior.
findings.finding: The runbook asserts that `fetch-secrets.sh` 'materializes [OAuth credentials] into the container `.env`' and that '`compose-up.sh` writes the `client-id` into `site/etc/gerrit.config` and the `client-secret` into `site/etc/secure.config`', but the actual scripts contain no such code. `fetch-secrets.sh` fetches exactly four params (none OAuth-related) and `compose-up.sh` only copies `gerrit.config` from the repo; neither writes `secure.config`.
findings.location: infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md Step 2; infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh; infra/scripts/compose-up.sh

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh` with a pinned-URL + sha256-verified install block for `oauth.jar` (parallel to the `events-log` block), and fill in the sha256 in `plugins/README.md` once the jar is fetched.
findings.finding: install-plugins.sh has no logic to download, verify, or install the oauth.jar for gerrit-oauth-provider; the sha256 in plugins/README.md is marked 'TBD — record on fetch'. The plan's in-scope AC ('wired into the plugin install path') is unfulfilled: a fresh redeploy would not install the OAuth plugin, and there is no sha256-enforced integrity check equivalent to the events-log pattern.
findings.location: Deliverables section 'install-plugins scaffolding' / AC: 'gerrit-oauth-provider pinned-jar provenance in plugins/README.md + install-plugins scaffolding' / infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh / infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md line 13
findings.suggested_fix: Add env vars `OAUTH_JAR_URL` and `OAUTH_JAR_SHA256` (with pinned defaults) to install-plugins.sh, download to a temp file, sha256-verify (fail on mismatch), then `mv` to `$GERRIT_SITE/plugins/oauth.jar`. Update the table in plugins/README.md with the concrete sha256 once fetched.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` to fetch `github_oauth_client_id` and `github_oauth_client_secret` from SSM, and update (or document the extension to) `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` to inject client-id into `site/etc/gerrit.config` and client-secret into `site/etc/secure.config` as part of the automated boot path.
findings.finding: fetch-secrets.sh does not fetch the new OAuth credentials (github_oauth_client_id, github_oauth_client_secret) from SSM. The runbook states 'fetch-secrets.sh materializes these into the container .env; compose-up.sh writes the client-id into site/etc/gerrit.config and the client-secret into site/etc/secure.config', but the script's SSM fetch list has no entries for these params and no logic to write them to gerrit.config or secure.config.
findings.location: Runbook Step 2 / infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh lines 56-59 / Deliverables 'In-repo' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `github_oauth_client_id` and `github_oauth_client_secret` to `fetch-secrets.sh`'s fetch list and `.env` output, extend `compose-up.sh` to write `site/etc/secure.config` from those env vars (mode 0600), and add both params to `ssm.tf`'s `rebar_secret_params` — or explicitly scope these as manual operator steps in the runbook and remove the claim that the existing scripts handle them.
findings.finding: The runbook asserts that `fetch-secrets.sh` will materialize the two new OAuth SSM params and that `compose-up.sh` will write `client-id` into `site/etc/gerrit.config` and `client-secret` into `site/etc/secure.config` — but neither script does this. `fetch-secrets.sh` fetches exactly 4 hardcoded params and writes only `infra/compose/.env`; `compose-up.sh` copies only `gerrit.config` (no `secure.config` logic). The new params (`github_oauth_client_id`, `github_oauth_client_secret`) are also absent from `ssm.tf`'s `rebar_secret_params` list. The described SSM→secure.config integration pipeline does not exist and would require new scripting.
findings.location: Runbook Step 2 (lines 41-43); infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh; infra/scripts/compose-up.sh lines 73-74; infra/terraform/ssm.tf
findings.suggested_fix: In `fetch-secrets.sh`, add `github_oauth_client_id=$(get_param github_oauth_client_id)` and `github_oauth_client_secret=$(get_param github_oauth_client_secret)` and emit them to `.env`. In `compose-up.sh`, after copying `gerrit.config`, add a block that reads those vars from `.env` and writes `site/etc/secure.config` with mode 0600 — mirroring how `materialize-deploy-key.sh` handles the deploy key. Add both SSM param names to `ssm.tf`'s `rebar_secret_params` list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `github_oauth_client_id` and `github_oauth_client_secret` to `fetch-secrets.sh` (and `ssm.tf`), and add logic to `compose-up.sh` (or a new `materialize-oauth-config.sh`) to write `client-id` into `site/etc/gerrit.config` and `client-secret` into `site/etc/secure.config` (mode 0600, owned by gerrit uid), with a fail-loud guard when `auth.type = OAUTH` is active but the secret is absent.
findings.finding: The runbook (Step 2) asserts that `compose-up.sh` will write the OAuth `client-secret` into `site/etc/secure.config` from SSM, but the committed `compose-up.sh` and `fetch-secrets.sh` contain no such logic — neither script references `github_oauth_client_secret`, writes to `secure.config`, or adds it to the `.env`. The secret materializiation path exists only as prose; without code, the secret either won't reach `secure.config` (Gerrit fails to boot with OAUTH) or must be placed manually (bypassing SSM, creating plaintext-on-disk risk).
findings.location: Runbook Step 2 (`infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md` lines 41-44) vs `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` and `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh`
findings.suggested_fix: In `fetch-secrets.sh`, add `github_oauth_client_secret="$(get_param github_oauth_client_secret)"` and expose it as `GERRIT_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET` in the `.env`. In `compose-up.sh`, after seeding `gerrit.config`, add a conditional block: if `grep -q 'type = OAUTH' ...gerrit.config`; then write `[plugin "gerrit-oauth-provider-github-oauth"]\nclient-secret = ${GERRIT_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET}` into `${SITE_HOST_DIR}/etc/secure.config` at mode 0600; fi. Add the two SSM param names to `ssm.tf`'s `rebar_secret_params` list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a pre-cutover spike: stand up a local/Docker Gerrit 3.14 instance, install the pinned gerrit-oauth-provider jar, configure a test GitHub OAuth App (or mock), and verify the plugin loads healthy and the OAuth redirect flow completes before committing to the live cutover.
findings.finding: The gerrit-oauth-provider plugin is described as 'actively maintained (3.13.x, tracks the Gerrit release train)' but the live Gerrit is 3.14 — a version gap that is asserted compatible without any validation step (smoke test, local Docker run, or compatibility check against the plugin release page) before the live cutover.
findings.location: Why section: 'actively maintained (3.13.x, tracks the Gerrit release train)'; Deliverables › In-repo: 'gerrit-oauth-provider pinned-jar provenance'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a runbook prerequisite: 'Validated on local Gerrit 3.14 Docker instance — plugin loads (check `gerrit plugin ls` output shows gerrit-oauth-provider ENABLED) and OAuth redirect to github.com/login/oauth/authorize succeeds.' Use `docker run -it gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14-rc` or the exact production image to replicate the environment.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `gerrit-oauth-provider` 3.14 artifact: locate the exact GerritForge CI URL (or GitHub release tag) for the `stable-3.14` build, record the specific version (not '3.14.x') and its sha256 in `plugins/README.md`, correct the plan overview's '3.13.x' reference, and extend `install-plugins.sh` with a fetch+verify block for `oauth.jar` matching the events-log pattern.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the `gerrit-oauth-provider` jar is available for Gerrit 3.14 and will be pinned with a verified sha256, but the provenance table in `plugins/README.md` explicitly marks the sha256 as 'TBD — record on fetch', no download URL (GerritForge CI job or GitHub release tag) is provided, and `install-plugins.sh` has not been extended to fetch/verify `oauth.jar`. The plan's WS8 overview also references '3.13.x' while the host runs 3.14.1.
findings.location: infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md (row 3 of plugin table); infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh; Plan overview 'actively maintained (3.13.x)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a second block to `install-plugins.sh` (parameterised via `OAUTH_JAR_URL` / `OAUTH_JAR_SHA256` env vars, defaulting to the pinned values) mirroring the events-log fetch-and-verify pattern. For the URL, check https://gerrit-ci.gerritforge.com/job/plugin-gerrit-oauth-provider-bazel-master-stable-3.14/ (the GerritForge naming convention) or the GitHub Releases page for `davido/gerrit-oauth-provider`; record the confirmed sha256 in the README table and remove the 'TBD' marker.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `github_oauth_client_id` and `github_oauth_client_secret` (or their SSM leaf names) to the `PARAMS` array in `infra/terraform/user_data.sh` alongside the `ssm.tf` update, as the ssm.tf comment explicitly requires these two files to stay in sync.
findings.finding: `infra/terraform/user_data.sh` contains a parallel hardcoded SSM-parameter-to-env-var mapping (the `PARAMS` associative array) that is never mentioned in the plan. If the new `github_oauth_client_id` and `github_oauth_client_secret` SSM params need to be read at cloud-init (fresh-instance) time, `user_data.sh` is also an unenumerated consumer that must be updated alongside `ssm.tf`.
findings.location: infra/terraform/user_data.sh lines 88-96; Deliverables / Access-gated AC: 'add the params to `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document a break-glass procedure in the runbook for GitHub OAuth outages (e.g., temporary reversion to DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT on a private network, or a pre-created Gerrit admin HTTP password for an emergency account).
findings.finding: GitHub OAuth is introduced as a hard external dependency for ALL human logins, but the plan has no acknowledgment or mitigation of GitHub-outage blast radius — if GitHub is unavailable, every human login to Gerrit fails with no fallback or degraded mode.
findings.location: Config (the target state) / Acceptance Criteria — Live
findings.suggested_fix: Pre-create a Gerrit admin account with an HTTP password stored in SSM as an emergency break-glass credential, documented in the runbook, so human admin access survives a GitHub outage.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and record a concrete pre-built artifact URL for the `gerrit-oauth-provider` 3.14 jar (e.g. a GerritForge CI URL or a GitHub Actions artifact from the davido repo), or explicitly document that a Bazel build is required and add build instructions to the runbook; update `plugins/README.md` with the pinned URL and sha256.
findings.finding: The `gerrit-oauth-provider` jar download URL for Gerrit 3.14 is unverified: `plugins/README.md` records only the GitHub repo homepage (not a specific pre-built artifact URL) and explicitly marks the sha256 as 'TBD — record on fetch'. Unlike `events-log` — which has a pinned GerritForge CI artifact URL with a verified sha256 — it is unconfirmed whether a pre-built 3.14 jar is available for download or whether the operator must build from source via Bazel. If a Bazel build is required, the runbook's Step 3 ('fetch the jar') significantly understates the operator effort.
findings.location: infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md line 13; Deliverables section 'gerrit-oauth-provider pinned-jar provenance'; Runbook Step 3
findings.suggested_fix: Check `https://gerrit-ci.gerritforge.com/job/plugin-gerrit-oauth-provider-bazel-master-stable-3.14/` (the GerritForge CI pattern used for other plugins) for a pre-built artifact. If found, record the URL + sha256 in `plugins/README.md` and add it to `install-plugins.sh` with the same `curl + sha256sum` pattern used for `events-log`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the specific pinned version (e.g., `gerrit-oauth-provider-3.14.0.jar`) and its SHA256 to the plan so the executor records them in `plugins/README.md` without making a design choice.
findings.finding: The plan states the jar should be 'pinned' but does not specify the exact version or SHA256 of the `gerrit-oauth-provider` jar to use with Gerrit 3.14 — an executing agent must choose the version, which is a real design decision (compatibility, security review) deferred to the executor.
findings.location: Deliverables section: 'gerrit-oauth-provider pinned-jar provenance in infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the runbook that the account-migration script is idempotent (e.g., skips accounts whose external IDs already contain a `github:` prefix) so it can be safely re-run after a partial failure.
findings.finding: The account-migration step (updating Gerrit NoteDb external IDs from DEVELOPMENT to GitHub OAuth identities) has no specified resumability or idempotency guarantee in the plan; if the migration script fails mid-way (e.g., after migrating 3 of 10 accounts), re-running it could double-apply changes or leave accounts in an inconsistent half-migrated state.
findings.location: Deliverables → In-repo → Runbook AC: 'account migration' step; 'Live (access-gated)' section
findings.suggested_fix: In the runbook's account-migration step, add a guard: before writing a new external ID mapping, check whether the account already has a `github:<username>` external ID and skip if present. Document this guard explicitly so operators know re-running is safe.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a pre-cutover smoke-test step in the runbook: stand up a staging/shadow Gerrit instance (or use a test account on the live instance while DEVELOPMENT mode is still active) to validate the plugin loads and OAuth redirect works before the final config flip.
findings.finding: The switch from DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT to auth.type=OAUTH is a single 100%-traffic flip on the live production Gerrit with no staged rollout, feature flag, or canary mechanism described.
findings.location: Deliverables — access-gated / Acceptance Criteria — Live
findings.suggested_fix: In the runbook, add a step: while still in DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT mode, install the plugin jar and verify it loads (check Gerrit plugin list endpoint), then test the OAuth redirect with a test GitHub account before flipping auth.type — this validates the plugin chain without affecting live users.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify what 'install-plugins scaffolding' means concretely (e.g., 'a shell script `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh` that copies the pinned jar into `$GERRIT_SITE/plugins/` and is called from the compose entrypoint') and restate the AC to match that artifact.
findings.finding: The deliverable 'install-plugins scaffolding' (and its AC counterpart 'wired into the plugin install path') is not defined as a concrete artifact type — an executing agent cannot determine whether to produce a Dockerfile COPY step, a compose entrypoint script, a Makefile target, or a bare volume-mount instruction, and cannot verify the result without knowing what 'wired' means.
findings.location: Deliverables section: 'install-plugins scaffolding' / AC: 'wired into the plugin install path'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'install-plugins scaffolding' with a concrete artifact, e.g.: 'Add `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh` (called from the Gerrit compose entrypoint) that downloads/copies the pinned `gerrit-oauth-provider-<version>.jar` into `$GERRIT_SITE/plugins/`.' Update the AC to: 'A plugin install script exists at `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh`, references the pinned jar SHA, and is invoked from the compose service definition.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add non-happy-path coverage to the runbook AC: document the expected Gerrit error page for denied/unrostered GitHub accounts, the recovery procedure for OAuth callback misconfiguration, and the session-invalidation behavior at cutover (warn active users or document expected session drop).
findings.finding: The OAuth non-happy-path for end users is absent from the plan: denied-access (GitHub account not on roster), OAuth callback misconfiguration errors, mid-session cutover invalidation, and abandoned OAuth flows are not addressed in the runbook AC or the verification step.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria → Live (access-gated) / Deliverables → Access-gated
findings.suggested_fix: In `infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md`, add a 'Failure modes' section covering: (1) user denied — expected Gerrit 'Sign In' error page + ACL remediation steps; (2) OAuth App misconfiguration — symptoms (redirect_uri_mismatch) + rollback trigger; (3) cutover session impact — note that active HTTP sessions will be invalidated and document whether a maintenance window is needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability step to the runbook: specify what log pattern or metric to alert on for OAuth auth failures (e.g., Gerrit error log for plugin exceptions, or an HTTP-level health check on the OAuth callback endpoint).
findings.finding: The plan introduces new auth failure points (GitHub OAuth calls, plugin load failures) but specifies no observability — no metric, log-alert, or health-check is mentioned that would allow operators to detect auth failures or GitHub OAuth degradation.
findings.location: Deliverables — In-repo / Acceptance Criteria

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR (e.g. `docs/adr/0001-gerrit-github-oauth.md`) capturing the decision to use GitHub OAuth + gerrit-oauth-provider, alternatives considered, and the chosen rationale.
findings.finding: The plan introduces GitHub OAuth (via gerrit-oauth-provider plugin) as the production auth pattern — a novel architectural decision — but does not include an ADR or CLAUDE.md update capturing the decision, alternatives considered (LDAP, OpenID Connect, SSO proxy), and the chosen rationale. The runbook covers procedure but not architectural context.
findings.location: Deliverables — In-repo / infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/0001-gerrit-github-oauth.md` with: context (DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT is dev-only), alternatives (LDAP, OpenID Connect direct, reverse-proxy org gate), decision (gerrit-oauth-provider GitHub backend), and consequences (GitHub uptime dependency, no reverse-proxy gate).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback procedure to the plan (not just to a future runbook): specify the exact steps to revert to DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT, how to handle accounts that have already acquired OAuth external-ids, and whether a gerrit.db/site backup snapshot is required before cutover.
findings.finding: The rollback path is deferred entirely to the runbook content (not yet written), and the plan does not specify what rollback actually means once accounts have been migrated from DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT to GitHub OAuth identities — reverting gerrit.config re-enables dev-auth but OAuth-linked accounts may be orphaned or fail to match, leaving the system in an inconsistent state.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria › In-repo: 'infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md documents … verify → rollback' and Deliverables › Handoff block
findings.suggested_fix: Before the live flip, take a snapshot of the Gerrit site directory (or at minimum `$GERRIT_SITE/db` and `etc/`). The rollback steps are: (1) stop Gerrit, (2) restore `gerrit.config` with `auth.type = DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT`, (3) optionally remove the plugin jar, (4) restart Gerrit. Document that OAuth external-ids added during the window are harmless under dev-auth (Gerrit will fall back to username matching) — or if they are not harmless, specify `gerrit gsql` commands to remove them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a pre-migration checkpoint to the runbook: record (or `git tag`) the `refs/meta/external-ids` SHA before any account-migration writes, so the rollback step can `git update-ref` it back to the pre-migration state.
findings.finding: The plan provides no explicit data-preservation rollback path specifically for the account-migration step: if the auth-type is flipped to OAUTH and then rolled back, accounts that had their external IDs rewritten for GitHub OAuth will not automatically revert, potentially leaving them inaccessible under the restored DEVELOPMENT mode.
findings.location: Deliverables → In-repo → Runbook AC: 'rollback' step; 'Live (access-gated)' → auth flip
findings.suggested_fix: In the runbook's pre-flight section, add: `git -C $GERRIT_SITE/git/All-Users.git rev-parse refs/meta/external-ids > /tmp/ext-ids-pre-migration.sha` and document the matching rollback command `git update-ref refs/meta/external-ids $(cat /tmp/ext-ids-pre-migration.sha)`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document in the runbook that the migration window requires either a brief Gerrit maintenance mode (disable new account creation) or a post-cutover check that scans for accounts lacking a `github:` external ID and flags them for manual linking.
findings.finding: The plan does not address accounts created or modified in the window between when the account-migration script runs and when `auth.type = OAUTH` is activated; users or bots that create accounts during this window may have their external IDs in the old format and fail to authenticate after the cutover.
findings.location: Deliverables → 'Live (access-gated)' section; Runbook AC

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
